name: libSQL
description: libSQL is an open-source, embeddable SQL database engine forked from SQLite by the Turso team. It is fully compatible with the SQLite file format and SQL dialect while adding modern features: native vector search for AI and RAG workloads, async I/O for non-blocking operations, WASM support for browser and edge environments, and embedded replicas that sync with remote databases. Written in Rust and C, libSQL is designed for applications that want SQLite's simplicity but need vector search, replication, or WebAssembly support.
core_strengths: Full SQLite file format and SQL dialect compatibility
Native vector search for AI embeddings and RAG pipelines
Async I/O for non-blocking queries in modern runtimes
WASM support for browser, edge, and serverless environments
Embedded replicas with remote sync for offline-first apps
best_use_cases: AI/ML applications needing local vector similarity search
Offline-first apps that sync with a cloud database
Browser and edge computing with WASM-compiled SQL
Mobile and desktop apps that embed a SQL database
Serverless functions requiring fast local SQL access
remarks: The Python libsql-experimental package supports Linux and macOS only; not currently available on Windows. Async I/O and embedded replicas require the libsql server (sqld) for sync. Vector search requires loading the libsql-vector extension at runtime. WASM support is a separate build target — not all features are available in-browser.
